title: Half-Open File Hammer
setup: The c-file is empty between your rook and the 8th rank, and Black's pawn shield has no escape window.
hint: Charge up the open c-file — the {{f7}}-{{g7}}-{{h7}} pawns trap the king for you.
explanation:
## The Idea

A **back-rank mate via a half-open file**. Even with extra material on the board, all that matters is the bare 8th rank and the locked-in pawn shield.

Play {{Rc8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{f8}}, {{h8}} and the rest of the 8th rank — covered by the rook on c8
- {{f7}}, {{g7}}, {{h7}} — all blocked by Black's own pawns
- Black has no piece that can interpose between c8 and g8 in time
- The king cannot capture: c8 is too far from g8

> **Takeaway:** A *half-open file* (yours has a pawn, theirs doesn't) is one of the most powerful structures in chess. Always check whether your rook can convert that file into a mate.
